summary: Matched the 96-byte fighter common function ftCo_800BEFD4 by moving it out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new source/header pair, src/melee/ft/ft_0BEF.c and src/melee/ft/ft_0BEF.h. The C implementation calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallIce, Ft_MF_None, start frame 0, anim speed 1, blend 0, and NULL, then sets Fighter bitfields x2219_b2 and x2219_b1 to true. The PR also removed the now-redundant local sdata2 float constants from the asm slice, added the new object to obj_files.mk, moved the function declaration out of ft_0BEC.h, and included ft_0BEF.h where ftdemo.c needs the symbol.
what_changed: Removed the hand-written assembly block for ftCo_800BEFD4 from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Removed ftCo_804D8C10 and ftCo_804D8C14 sdata2 float constants from the asm file because the matched C source now supplies the equivalent 0 and 1 arguments directly.
Added src/melee/ft/ft_0BEF.h declaring ftCo_800BEFD4 with a GALE01_0BEFD4 include guard and placeholder.h include.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ft_0BEF.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Removed the ftCo_800BEFD4 declaration from src/melee/ft/ft_0BEC.h and included ft/ft_0BEF.h from src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c.
smart_moves: Kept object ordering in obj_files.mk aligned with the original text layout by placing ft_0BEF.c.o immediately before asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o, preserving addresses while peeling one matched function out of the asm file.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and named Fighter bitfields x2219_b2 and x2219_b1 instead of reproducing raw byte/rlwimi manipulation, making the decomp clearer while matching the original behavior.
Translated the assembly constants into semantically meaningful Fighter_ChangeMotionState arguments: ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallIce, Ft_MF_None, numeric 0/1/0, and NULL.
Split the declaration into a new ft_0BEF.h instead of leaving ftCo_800BEFD4 in ft_0BEC.h, reflecting the new source-file ownership.
decomp_lessons: Small fighter common functions can often be matched by identifying the motion-state change call, then mapping post-call bit twiddles to known Fighter bitfields.
When migrating one function out of a shared asm file, remove only that function and any constants used solely by it; leave adjacent asm labels and constants intact.
After creating a new C file for a matched slice, update both obj_files.mk ordering and local includes so downstream users include the new owning header.
A 100% match report for a tiny function is still useful when it retires assembly and documents semantic names for motion IDs, flags, and fighter bitfields.
naming_conventions: Function retained the address-based common fighter name ftCo_800BEFD4.
New source/header pair used the address-range style ft_0BEF.c and ft_0BEF.h.
Header guard used GALE01_0BEFD4, matching the function address rather than the file basename.
Motion-state enum name ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallIce and flag name Ft_MF_None were preferred over raw integer arguments.
Fighter state bits were expressed as x2219_b2 and x2219_b1, matching existing bitfield-style naming for unknown fighter flags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mapped assembly li r4, 9 to ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallIce in the Fighter_ChangeMotionState call.
Mapped zero and one float loads from ftCo_804D8C10 and ftCo_804D8C14 to C arguments 0 and 1.
Mapped final NULL argument to the zero r6 parameter in the seven-argument Fighter_ChangeMotionState call signature.
Mapped two rlwimi writes to byte offset 0x2219 into assignments fp->x2219_b2 = true and fp->x2219_b1 = true.
Removed matching assembly after adding the C object before the remaining asm object, a standard incremental decomp tactic for preserving link order.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document Fighter bitfields x2219_b1 and x2219_b2.
Find other ft_0BEF or ft_08A4.s peel-off PRs that use the same object-ordering pattern.
Check whether GALE01_0BEFD4 header guards were later normalized to file-based guards.
